source: Stage 2 clinical planning notes and clinician-provided rules; requires formal clinical review before validated use.
medical_boundary: Decision-support visualization only; not a surgical instruction.

principles.label: RSTL first
principles.description: Place the incision long axis parallel to the local relaxed skin tension line when no sensitive-structure exception applies.

principles.label: Natural crease secondary
principles.description: Use natural creases or wrinkles as a secondary scar-camouflage cue when available.

principles.label: Aesthetic subunit boundary secondary
principles.description: Use aesthetic subunit boundaries as a secondary cue when they reduce visual disruption.

principles.label: Sensitive free-margin exception
principles.description: Near lower eyelid, vermilion border, nasal ala, and other free margins, protect function and contour before following RSTL.

warnings: Forehead landmarks are sparse; require manual confirmation in high hairline or poor capture cases.
forehead.clinician_note: Prefer forehead RSTL/crease direction; do not infer hairline margin suitability automatically.

warnings: Ear and periauricular landmarks are weak in the current face mesh classifier.
ear_region.clinician_note: Require manual confirmation around preauricular and postauricular boundaries.

warnings: Check eyelid position and canthal support before accepting any candidate.
periorbital.clinician_note: RSTL is only acceptable after free-margin risk is reviewed.

warnings: Upper eyelid crease and lid margin require clinician confirmation.
upper_eyelid.clinician_note: Use eyelid crease and functional lid assessment before accepting RSTL-only direction.

warnings: Risk of lower-lid retraction, ectropion, or lid-globe separation; require clinician override before commit.
lower_eyelid.clinician_note: Protect the free margin and lid support before following RSTL.

warnings: Medial canthal contour and lacrimal region require manual review.
inner_canthus.clinician_note: Do not rely on broad RSTL alone near the medial canthus.

warnings: Nasal dorsum/root boundaries are approximate in the current classifier.
nasal_dorsum.clinician_note: Nasal subunit boundaries may be preferable to local RSTL in selected cases.

warnings: Risk of alar displacement or contour distortion; require clinician confirmation.
nasal_ala.clinician_note: Subunit boundaries and alar support may override local RSTL.

warnings: Risk of nasal tip contour distortion; require clinician confirmation.
nasal_tip.clinician_note: Nasal tip support and subunit boundaries can override local RSTL.

warnings: Check proximity to nasolabial fold, lower eyelid, and oral commissure manually.
cheek.clinician_note: Cheek candidates can usually follow local RSTL unless a nearby free margin changes the priority.

warnings: Confirm fold position and oral traction before accepting the candidate.
nasolabial_fold.clinician_note: Nasolabial fold can be a secondary scar-camouflage cue.

warnings: Lateral face and temple boundaries are approximate in the current classifier.
temple_cheek.clinician_note: Crow's-feet and lateral subunit boundaries can be useful scar-camouflage cues.

warnings: Risk of vermilion mismatch, notching, or oral commissure traction; require clinician confirmation.
lip_vermilion.clinician_note: Border alignment and functional contour can override RSTL.

warnings: Check philtrum, white roll, and vermilion proximity manually.
upper_lip.clinician_note: White lip subunits and vermilion alignment may override local RSTL.

warnings: Risk of oral commissure traction, notching, or asymmetry.
oral_commissure.clinician_note: Commissure alignment and functional contour can override RSTL.

warnings: Check mental crease, lower lip traction, and mandibular border proximity manually.
chin.clinician_note: Mental crease can be a secondary direction cue when it improves scar camouflage.

warnings: Jawline and neck transition are approximate; confirm mandibular border manually.
jawline.clinician_note: Mandibular border/subunit contour may be a secondary guide.

sensitive_regions.lower_eyelid: Protect the lower eyelid free margin; consider manual override away from vertical traction.
sensitive_regions.lip_vermilion: Protect vermilion border alignment; require clinician confirmation before committing.
sensitive_regions.nasal_ala: Protect nasal alar contour; evaluate distortion risk before accepting.
sensitive_regions.nasal_tip: Protect nasal tip contour and support; require clinician confirmation before committing.
sensitive_regions.oral_commissure: Protect oral commissure alignment and traction; require clinician confirmation before committing.

lower_eyelid.reason: Prefer a clinician-confirmed axis that protects lower eyelid support before following local RSTL.

lip_vermilion.reason: Protect vermilion alignment; subunit border alignment may override local RSTL.

oral_commissure.reason: Protect oral commissure symmetry and avoid unreviewed traction vectors.

nasal_ala.reason: Protect alar contour; nasal subunit boundary can override local RSTL.

nasal_tip.reason: Protect nasal tip contour and support before following local RSTL.
